Empires & Puzzles Costumed Ana-Belle Review: The Baroness of Dust

Ana-Belle's new 'Baroness of Dust' costume trades the feather duster for a mafia makeover. With significantly boosted stats and a higher healing potential, she's back to remind the meta that sometimes, the only way to win is to reset the board completely.
Costumed Ana-Belle Review: The Mafia Maid Who Cleans Up The Evidence
Costumed Ana-Belle: The Mafia Maid Who Cleans Up The Evidence

Ana-Belle has ditched the humble housekeeping attire for something far more sinister. As the "Baroness of Dust," she joins the Mafia-themed costume lineup of 2025, bringing updated stats and a refined version of her signature board-wiping utility. While the core mechanic remains the same—delete everything, heal everyone—the Costume pushes her stats into modern territory, making her a viable 4-star option even in 5-star wars. She is no longer just a utility tool; she's a sturdy, high-healing anchor.

The "Baroness of Dust" look is fantastic. Gone is the playful maid outfit; in its place is a sharp, elegant, mob-wife aesthetic with a gothic twist. She looks less like she’s going to dust your shelves and more like she’s going to "dust"
. The darker tones and confident pose reflect her upgraded power level. It’s a serious glow-up that fits the current darker tone of the Halloween event.
This is the upgrade we were waiting for. With
(before Limit Breaks), Costume Ana-Belle jumps over 90 points ahead of her base version.

Her stats are now comparable to the 4-star heavy hitters of late 2024/2025. Her 791 Defense and 1517 Health allow her to actually take a hit from a modern sniper without disintegrating. This durability is critical for her role as a "reset" button—you can't reset the board if you're dead.
"Dress Up!" is a subtle but powerful tweak to her original "Tidy Up!" skill.
The base heal drops slightly to 43% (from 45%), but the potential scaling increases to
(from 65%). Each removed status effect now adds

This change incentivizes using her exactly when she is needed most: when the board is an absolute disaster. If your team is covered in burns and the enemy has attack buffs, she essentially becomes a full healer (73% is practically 100% for damaged heroes).
It still retains the "Dispel/Cleanse ALL" mechanic. It wipes the slate clean. No more buffs for anyone. No more ailments for anyone. Just health.
. This is an absurd amount of healing for a 4-star hero. It rivals 5-star healers like Ariel or heavy boosted healers.
To beat her base version's healing (45% + 2% per effect), the Costume (43% + 3% per effect) only needs to remove
. Since you are almost always removing more than 2 effects (between 5 heroes on each side), the Costume is mathematically superior in 99% of combat scenarios.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Morlovia family bonus (Mana Gen) is still excellent. Combined with the Costume Bonus (which usually adds 5% mana gen), she is very easy to speed up.
The 65% chance to steal a buff remains. As with the base version, this is quirky because she often dispels the buff she just stole. However, the fallback
is reliable chip damage when opening a fight against a clean enemy team.
The original Ana-Belle was a niche favorite. The Costume elevates her from "niche tool" to "meta contender" in the 4-star bracket.
By pushing her stats to the ~775 range, Small Giant has ensured she remains relevant for another 2-3 years. She can now stand toe-to-toe with the Construct and Opera heroes.

In Buff Booster, she denies the enemy. In Rush, she resets the board. In Bloody Battle... well, she cleanses, which is still useful. She is arguably the best "pure" healer (non-booster) in the 4-star rarity right now.
Can resist mana shield. Good for survival.

Can inflict Delay (mana gen drop) on enemies she hits with tiles or her passive damage. Since she deals damage to ALL if no buffs are present, the Sorcerer class actually has synergy with her passive! If she opens the fight and deals 150% damage, she can apply Delay to multiple enemies.
The costume bonus (Att/Def/HP/Mana) makes the Base version tankier too if you choose to use it, but the Costume's higher base stats + higher healing ceiling make it the clear winner.
With updated stats, she can afford to be a bit more aggressive, but she is still a healer.
Ensures she survives the alpha strikes. Her job is to reset the game; she can't do that if she's dead.
Synergizes with the Sorcerer class talent (Delay) and her passive (150% damage).
. Let your snipers do the damage; she is there to clean up the mess.
The scaling to 73% is unmatched in her rarity.
She doesn't feel like a "glass cannon healer" anymore.
The class change actually works with her passive damage, unlike the Cleric class which was purely defensive.
Still dispels your own buffs. This hasn't changed.
In a meta dominated by boosted health (Ptolemy, etc.), pure healing can feel "capped."
To get max healing, you need a board full of effects. If the board is clean, her healing is just "okay" (43%).
A viable healer for your 4th-6th flags, especially against heavy ailment defenses (e.g., teams relying on multiple DoTs).
Great for stages with bosses that stack buffs.
Let the enemy cast their buffs/ailments. Tank the hits. Then fire Ana-Belle C. She heals you to full, strips the enemy naked, and your snipers finish them off.
Pair with other Sorcerers or mana controllers. If her passive triggers the Sorcerer delay, you can slow down the enemy team significantly without using a mana control special.
This is a jackpot pull. A 4-star hero with 5-star healing potential? Max her immediately. She will carry you through Season maps (cleansing annoying boss buffs) and Raids.

You might have benched the original Ana-Belle because she died too easily. Unbench her. The Costume gives her the bulk she was missing. While she doesn't overheal like Ptolemy or give Growth, her Dispel All is a unique mechanic that solves problems Goldie can't (like heavy enemy buffs).
"The art alone is worth the pull. She looks like she runs the place."
"Sorcerer on a healer? Weird... oh wait, the passive damage! smart."
"Finally, stats that match her utility. She was too squishy before."
The Baroness of Dust is a masterclass in how to update an old hero. Small Giant didn't break her mechanic; they just made it
and gave her the stats to use it. She is elegant, deadly, and effective. Whether you need to wipe a Ludwig's taunt or cleanse a Quintus's ailments, she does it with style.
One of the best 4-star costumes available. A must-level for anyone serious about tournaments or war depth.
Review of the new Costume Ana-Belle (Baroness of Dust). With upgraded stats and a higher healing ceiling, is this 4-star healer now a 5-star in disguise?
